title: Eppstein Open Problem 1 (Linear characterisation)
statement_text: Characterise the subgraphs occurring $O(n)$ times in graphs of given genus.
context_text: After proving Perles' conjecture independently, Eppstein [18] posed this open problem in 1993 asking for a structural characterisation of which fixed graphs $H$ satisfy $C(H, \Sigma, n) = O(n)$ for graphs embedded in a surface $\Sigma$. This paper answers the question as a special case of the main theorem.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.2 shows $C(H, \Sigma, n) = \Theta(n^{f(H)})$ where $f(H)$ is the flap-number; a subgraph $H$ occurs $O(n)$ times if and only if $f(H) \leq 1$, equivalently if and only if $H$ is not strongly non-planar.
notes: PDF source; stated as a bullet-point attributed problem, not a labelled theorem environment. Eppstein's year 1993 is stated explicitly in the paper.

title: Eppstein Open Problem 2 (Nonlinear characterisation)
statement_text: Characterise the subgraphs occurring a number of times which is a nonlinear function of $n$ in graphs of given genus.
context_text: Eppstein [18] posed this companion open problem in 1993 alongside the linear characterisation question, asking which fixed graphs $H$ yield superlinear counts $C(H, \Sigma, n)$ in graphs embedded in a surface. This paper answers the question completely.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.2 shows $C(H, \Sigma, n) = \Theta(n^{f(H)})$; a subgraph $H$ occurs a nonlinear number of times if and only if its flap-number satisfies $f(H) \geq 2$.
notes: PDF source; stated as a bullet-point attributed problem, not a labelled theorem environment. Eppstein's year 1993 is stated explicitly in the paper.
